resources.string: Use pure black background for dark themes
Use the Android system typeface across mpvRx instead of Google Sans Flex
Only available on Android 12 and above
Show full folder and video names without truncation
Hide the background of all player control buttons
Show label for new unplayed videos
Display a \"New\" label on videos added within specified days that have never been played
Days threshold for new video label
Videos added within the last %d days without playback history will be marked as new
Display generated thumbnails in video lists and folders
Choose how thumbnails are generated, including embedded artwork or a specific frame position
This will clear cached thumbnails. New thumbnails will be generated with the selected strategy as you browse.
Maximum thumbnail resolution: %1$d px. Higher quality uses more storage and may generate more slowly.
Capture the frame at %1$d%% of the video
Generate thumbnails for network streams (May increase data usage on mobile networks)
Show or hide Home in the bottom navigation bar
Show or hide Recents in the bottom navigation bar
Show or hide Playlists in the bottom navigation bar
Show or hide Network in the bottom navigation bar
Automatically scroll to the last played video when opening video lists
Mark video as watched when playback reaches %d%%
%1$s skipped per navigation step for single-child folders
Used for the next link or when the current link is reloaded.
Show next/previous buttons for all videos in folder
Automatically play next video when current ends
Stay on current video when it ends
Automatically enter PIP mode when pressing home or back
Screen stays awake while video is paused
Screen can turn off while video is paused
Resume video automatically after unlocking if it was playing before the screen locked
Keep video paused after the screen is unlocked
Show buffered range on seek bar
Display the online buffer ahead of the playhead on the seek bar.
Show chapter indicators on seek bar
Separate chapters visually along the seek bar.
Export settings to an XML file
Import settings from an XML file
Load scripts from your MPV configuration directory
All customized player control layouts will be reset to default positions
Duration to skip forward on custom skip button
Show a fast thumbnail bubble while scrubbing. Turn off for legacy live video seeking.
Close after the end of playback
Swipe right-to-left to seek forward, left-to-right to seek backward
Swipe left or right on screen to seek forward or backward
Swipe up in center for playlist
Adjust how much distance is covered per swipe
Show system status bar with controls
Use single tap for center gesture
Use a single tap in the center for gestures (like play/pause) instead of a double tap.
Hold in the center, then drag up or down to move subtitle position

You can bind custom actions to these gestures in input.conf using the following key codes:

Left: MBTN_LEFT_DBL
Center: MBTN_MID_DBL
Right: MBTN_RIGHT_DBL

You can bind custom actions to media controls in input.conf using the following key codes:

Previous: PREV
Play/Pause: PLAYPAUSE
Next: NEXT
When enabled, tapping on thumbnails enters selection mode. When disabled, tapping thumbnails plays the video.
Enabling gpu-next may cause purple screen issues during playback on some devices.
If you experience purple screens, try to disable opengl-pbo in Advanced settings (mpv.conf):
Enable Vulkan rendering on supported devices (may improve performance)
May fix black screens on some video codecs, can also improve performance at the cost of quality
Search and download subtitles from Online sources
Choose a folder containing .ttf or .otf subtitle fonts
Automatically load external subtitles with the same name.
Delete all files in the current save location
Are you sure you want to delete all downloaded subtitles? This action cannot be undone.
Preferred languages, audio channels, pitch correction
Prevents the audio from becoming high-pitched at faster speeds and low-pitched at slower speeds
Automatically adjust audio volume to maintain consistent loudness levels
Audio language(s) to be selected by default on a video with multiple audio streams, Two- or three-letter languages codes work. Multiple values can be delimited by comma.
Restart mpvRx to apply the new language.
Dumps and copies the logs to be shared with the developers
Helps in debugging but may hinder performance
Are you sure you want to clear your playback history?
This will clear all your previous media\'s playback saved settings
Track and display recently played videos and playlists
Enable experimental or advanced features such as stats page and Anime4K upscaling
Anime4K requires either legacy gpu, or gpu-next with Vulkan enabled. gpu-next on OpenGL will fall back to legacy gpu for shader compatibility.
Cannot use Anime4K with gpu-next unless Vulkan is enabled
Adds line darkening after the main upscaler.
Adds line thinning after the main upscaler.
Adds the DoG deblur pass before darken/thin, costs more GPU time.
Overrides are inactive for ASS/SSA format. Toggle ASS Override to customize.
Scale subtitles by window size and use video margins
When ambient mode is on, draw subtitles with the video instead of over the ambient area
Some filters may not work on your current video driver
Overview. CPU/GPU usage, A/V sync, dropped frames, clock speed.
Frame timing. Decode/render times, scaler info.
Cache status. Buffer health, demuxer cache, seekable ranges.
Current keybindings. List of mapped keyboard shortcuts.
Track list. Details of video, audio and subtitle tracks.
Looks like %s experienced an unexpected error. Consider sharing the crash logs in a GitHub issue.
Database deleted successfully. Please restart the app.
This crash appears to be database-related. Try deleting the database to fix it.
Show ripple when double tap seeking
Enter language codes separated by commas (e.g., eng,jpn,spa)
Rescan the selected source folder and refresh the font list
Notification permission is required for background playback controls.
Notifications are disabled for mpvRx. Enable them to show playback controls.
When enabled, scans also include videos from folders hidden by .nomedia and their subfolders.
Tap the button below to add folders you want to hide from the folder list
This will remove all folders from the blacklist. You can add them back later.
Enter the URL of an M3U playlist file
Inset player controls and statistics around camera cutouts.
AI rename, subtitle translation, speech-to-text, offline models (OpenCode/Groq/OpenAI)
Choose how player controls appear and disappear
Select the transition when starting playback
Show next/previous buttons for all videos in folder
Play videos individually (select multiple for playlist)
Fetch intro, recap, outro, credits, and preview markers from the selected provider
Choose IntroDB, TIDB, AniSkip, or Anime Skip for online intro/outro markers
Analyze chapter name variants like opening/ending/credits and create skip markers
Automatically skip opening markers during playback
Automatically skip ending markers during playback
Display the Android system navigation bar over player controls
Show the vertical volume pill while swiping for volume
Show the vertical brightness pill while swiping for brightness
Show a simple speed label while long-pressing to boost playback speed
Show a brief pill when cycling aspect ratio (16:9, Fit, Crop…)
Show zoom percentage pill when pinching to zoom
Show pill when toggling repeat mode or shuffle
Show brief text pills from custom buttons, ambient toggle, subtitle drag, and Lua scripts
Show online provider error/status messages for Wyzie subtitle and marker lookups
Enter custom seek duration in seconds (1–120)
Enter custom hide time in milliseconds
Only show subtitles optimized for hearing impaired

Successfully imported: %1$s
Failed: %2$s
Version: %3$s

Please restart the app for all changes to take effect.
Tap to select - creates Subtitles/, Fonts/, scripts/, script-modules/ subdirs
Set storage location and enable scripts first
Create and manage script-powered player buttons
This will delete cached thumbnails from storage and memory.
Required for gated models. Get one from huggingface.co/settings/tokens
Models are ranked by how fast they feel after loading, RAM pressure, and subtitle translation quality.
Enable this to see the model\'s internal thought process for supported models
Use AI to generate clean filenames for bulk rename operations
Auto-format video titles for Wyzie/SubHub subtitle search
Generate subtitles from audio while playing video
Used for both real-time streaming and batch subtitle generation
Custom prompt will be used instead of built-in instructions
Built-in AI instructions will be used
Leave a field blank to use the built-in instruction for that task. If you have an older global prompt saved, it will be used as a fallback.
Instructions for formatting subtitle search queries…
Legacy global prompt saved. It will be used whenever a task-specific prompt is empty.
Subtitle translation can be a bit messy. For best results, use better models and don\'t rant that subs aren\'t working properly.
Fetch intro, recap, outro, credits, and preview markers from an online provider
Choose IntroDB, TIDB, AniSkip, or Anime Skip for online intro/outro markers
Create skip markers from chapter names such as opening, ending, credits, or preview
Skip opening markers automatically during playback
Skip ending markers automatically during playback
Root folder — auto-creates Subtitles/ and Fonts/ subdirs
AI-powered rename, subtitle formatting, speech-to-text, subtitle translation, offline models
Choose OpenCode, Groq, OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Together, or offline local models
Enter and verify your AI provider API key
Fetch and select which AI model to use
Show the model\'s internal thought process for supported models
Use AI to generate clean filenames for bulk rename operations
Auto-format video titles for Wyzie/SubHub subtitle search
Configure STT provider, real-time model, audio language, and output format
Translate external subtitles using AI with auto-translate target languages
Override default instructions for rename, translation, and formatting tasks
Download and manage local LLMs for fully offline AI features
Clear playback positions, selected subtitles, audio tracks, delays, and speeds
No recently played items to clear
Delete all cached video thumbnails (will regenerate as you browse folders)
You need to install yt-dlp to play this link (Settings > Advanced > yt-dlp Manager)
Increases compatibility on older devices but may limit quality
Use custom keywords for skipping openings
Enable user-defined keywords for opening matching instead of default ones
Use custom keywords for skipping endings
Enable user-defined keywords for ending matching instead of default ones
HDR10-style PQ with Astra tone mapping and Bottosson gamut mapping
HLG HDR through the hdr-toys Astra and Bottosson shader chain
BT.2020 / BT.1886 conversion using hdr-toys gamut mapping
High-quality mpv HDR output without hdr-toys shaders
This will reset the controls in this region to their default configuration.
Are you sure you want to delete \"%1$s\"? This action cannot be undone.
AI Rename is disabled. Enable it in Settings → AI Integration.
Play recently played or first video
Add SMB, FTP, or WebDAV connections to browse network files
Import a playlist from a web URL
mpvRx needs access to your video files to provide its core functionality as a media player.
The permission is used exclusively for:
• Discovering and displaying your video files\n• Playing media content\n• Loading subtitle files
mpvRx has always required storage access permission as it\'s essential for the app to find all media and subtitle files on your device, including the ones that are not supported by the system.
However, due to a change in security policy, apps built for Android 11 and above now require additional permission to continue accessing the same.
Please know that this permission is only used for the auto-discovery of media/subtitle files on your device and will not allow us to access the private data files stored by other apps in any way.
mpvRx is an open source project. You can review the source code and verify how permissions are used by visiting our GitHub repository at:
Be rest assured, your privacy is our utmost priority, and we neither access your files for other purposes nor transfer or store them to our servers. They remain safe on your device.
Newly enabled scripts can load during playback. Scripts you turn off may need the video to be reopened before they fully stop running.
Enable GPU Next and Vulkan before using HDR modes
If you enjoy mpvRx, consider supporting its development. Every bit helps!
Core open source dependencies used by mpvRx.
Install and update yt-dlp for streaming support
Instructions for formatting subtitle search queries...
Subtitle translation can be a bit messy.
When translating subtitles, if 1 language is configured it translates directly. If 2+ are configured, a picker appears.
Enable dual pane layout on tablets in folder view
Show audio files in the browser
Show OpenGL blob visualizer when playing audio
Keep audio and video playing when leaving the player or locking the screen
Long press to reorder items. Tap the \'-\' icon to remove them.
Tap buttons from the \'Available Palette\' below
All available buttons are in use.
Drag to reorder • Tap any slot to expand & edit
Backup or share all your custom buttons with their script code as an XML file
Save the script first before sharing
Show Media Info in system \"Open with\" / sharing menus to analyze files from other apps.
Uses mpv native lossless output; Android fallback uses lossless on Android 11+.
Use placeholders to customize the screenshot filename:\n
Download the latest wrapper modules and compile python-friendly native binaries inside local sandboxed folders.
Bitrate caps apply when yt-dlp reports audio bitrate metadata.
Automatically extract and load physical subtitle tracks from supported URLs.
Fetch auto-caption tracks (e.g. YouTube Speech-to-Text) when regular subs are absent.
Overrides app subtitle languages only for yt-dlp downloads.
Custom HTTP agent, proxy, extractor args, SponsorBlock, and raw options
Passed to yt-dlp as format-sort for advanced ranking.
Leave blank to use default browser User-Agent. Helps bypass anti-bot scrapers.
Ask yt-dlp to use its extractor-level region bypass logic.
Start live streams from the beginning when the extractor supports it.
Anything not exposed above. Separate options with new lines or commas; quote values that contain commas.
Declarative UI toolkit used across the app interface.
Navigation model used for app screens and flows.
Dependency injection for repositories, preferences, and view models.
Local database layer for playback state and cached metadata.
HTTP client for subtitle search and other network requests.
JSON serialization for API payloads and internal models.
Compose helpers for Android runtime permission flows.
Media inspection for codecs, subtitles, and technical metadata.
SMB/CIFS client support for network shares.
FTP and related network protocol support.
WebDAV client support for remote file access.
Embedded HTTP server components used by local streaming features.
Storage Access Framework helpers for file and tree operations.
Reads font metadata for subtitle font handling.
Preference UI components used across settings screens.
Scrollbar component for long Compose lists.
Drag-and-drop list reordering for Compose surfaces.
A customizable seekbar component for Jetpack Compose.
A powerful, efficient code editor for Android with TextMate highlighting.
Android media libraries for advanced playback and effects.
Java HTML Parser for web scraping and document extraction.
Local LLM inference bridge for running AI models on Android.
Core Kotlin extensions for Android framework APIs.
Backward-compatible versions of Android framework APIs.
Flexible layout manager for Android interfaces.
Abstraction layer for document storage access.
Android media session and playback APIs.
UI and storage for app preference screens.
Prefetches classes and methods for faster app startup.
Material Design components and theming library.
Material Symbols icon set for Compose.
libcurl static library for Android NDK.
Java 8+ API desugaring for older Android versions.
Soft, muted colors with gentle look
Vintage film look with soft focus
